title: Public Sector Governed Analytics — TRE + DP (P-07 pattern)
description: Accredited research access to sensitive administrative data within a Trusted Research Environment, with differential privacy applied at the statistical output release stage. Follows the ONS Secure Research Service / ADR UK pattern.

pet_components.role: institutional access controls and procedural governance — Five Safes framework gates who may access what data for which purposes; output checking is a first-class assurance mechanism

parameters.output_checking: human review by ONS output clearing team

pet_components.parameter_notes: TRE is the primary accountability mechanism. All outputs must pass safe outputs review regardless of DP application.

pet_components.role: formal statistical disclosure bound applied at output release — DP noise added to released statistics to bound individual-level leakage

parameters.sensitivity: query-specific (L1 global sensitivity analysis per output)
parameters.accounting_method: sequential composition with advanced composition for repeated queries

pet_components.parameter_notes: Pure DP (δ=0) chosen to maximise legal defensibility for statistical releases. ε=1.0 per query; total release budget governed by release policy. Parameter policy agreed with legal and statistical governance leads.

trust_model.summary: Data subjects are the general public — census respondents, admin data subjects. Approved researchers are trusted to operate within project scope under DUA. ONS infrastructure is trusted. TRE governance is the primary accountability mechanism; DP provides a mathematical second factor on statistical outputs.

threat_actors.capability: direct access to microdata within TRE; can run arbitrary queries within project scope
threat_actors.mitigated_by: TRE — Five Safes access controls, project scope limitations, output checking, audit logs
threat_actors.residual_risk: Residual inference risk from repeated queries within session; mitigated by DP composition accounting and human output checking

threat_actors.capability: may attempt to link released statistics with external datasets (electoral roll, published records)
threat_actors.mitigated_by: DP — formal bound on per-individual information released in statistics; output checking provides an additional layer
threat_actors.residual_risk: Auxiliary data attacks are not fully modelled — see auxiliary_data_risk in data profile. ε=1.0 provides strong protection but does not eliminate re-identification risk under very rich auxiliary data.

out_of_scope_threats: Compromise of ONS infrastructure or staff (modelled separately under ISMS)
Supply-chain attacks on approved DP tooling

required_artefacts.artefact: output clearance workflow and decision log (TRE layer)
required_artefacts.location: SRS output checking team — internal records system
required_artefacts.notes: All outputs cleared by named ONS output checker; decision rationale recorded

required_artefacts.artefact: DP accountant and parameter manifest (DP layer)
required_artefacts.notes: Sequential composition budget tracked per project; release policy sets maximum total epsilon

required_artefacts.artefact: access and project approval audit records
required_artefacts.location: ONS UKSA accreditation and project approval records

required_artefacts.notes: Advanced composition documented in DP parameter manifest

assurance_targets.maturity_justification: TRE + DP is operationalised with maintained change-control logs, third-party auditable artefacts, and a formal release policy. Full audit-readiness achieved where both layers produce maintained artefact sets.

governance_controls.access_control: Five Safes accreditation — safe people (approved researcher status), safe projects (project scope DUA), safe data (de-identified linkage), safe settings (TRE desktop), safe outputs (human output checking)
governance_controls.change_control: DP parameter changes require joint sign-off by statistical and legal governance leads; TRE policy changes subject to UKSA board review

frameworks_applied.notes: ONS SRS is the reference implementation of Five Safes in UK public sector
GOVERN, IDENTIFY, and CONTROL functions well-covered; COMMUNICATE partially

deployment_context.sub_sector: official statistics and accredited administrative data research

applicable_regulations.gap_notes: Art. 89 research exemption and statistical purpose basis documented

relevant_articles: Part 5 — data sharing for research and statistics

standards_alignment.notes: ε=1.0 per-query with advanced composition is consistent with NIST guidelines; legal sufficiency assessment documented separately
ONS Safe Outputs guidance is consistent with ICO anonymisation framework

tooling_candidates.name: OpenDP
tooling_candidates.notes: Reference implementation for DP statistics; used in academic and public sector settings

tooling_candidates.name: Tumult Analytics
tooling_candidates.notes: Used by U.S. Census Bureau DAS; strong composition accounting support

residual_risks.risk: Legal sufficiency of DP evidence varies by jurisdiction and regulatory context; ε=1.0 is not universally accepted as 'anonymisation' under data protection law
residual_risks.mitigation_path: Maintain legal opinion on DP + TRE combination; document that DP is used as a second-factor control alongside TRE governance, not as sole anonymisation mechanism

residual_risks.risk: Auxiliary data attacks not fully modelled; rich external datasets (voter rolls, social media) could in principle enable linkage attacks on released statistics
residual_risks.mitigation_path: Human output checking provides defence-in-depth; future: TAPAS-style adversarial audit of released outputs

change_log.change: Initial card created as YAPS example
change_log.author: Privacy Explorer
